blocks.goal: Create a dedicated interface holding terminal and terminal-like operations currently implemented as many default methods in IOStream, so these methods can be moved out of the large IOStream interface without breaking compilation.

ops.details: Create a new interface org.apache.commons.io.function.IOStreamTerminal<T> in the same package. The new interface will extend IOBaseStream<T, IOStream<T>, Stream<T>> and provide default implementations for terminal and terminal-like methods copied from IOStream: allMatch, anyMatch, collect(Collector), collect(IOSupplier, IOBiConsumer, IOBiConsumer), count, findAny, findFirst, forAll, forEach, forEachOrdered, toArray(), toArray(IntFunction), reduce variants (reduce(IOBinaryOperator), reduce(identity, IOBinaryOperator), reduce(identity, IOBiFunction, IOBinaryOperator)), max, min, noneMatch. The default implementations will be exact copies from IOStream so they rely on unwrap() from IOBaseStream and Erase helper methods already present in the project.

blocks.goal: Reduce the size of IOStream by delegating terminal methods to the newly created IOStreamTerminal interface and keep IOStream focused on stream transformation / construction utilities.

inputs: IOStream#collect(IOSupplier<R>, IOBiConsumer<R, ? super T>, IOBiConsumer<R, R>)
IOStream#reduce(U, IOBiFunction<U, ? super T, U>, IOBinaryOperator<U>)
outputs: IOStreamTerminal: these methods are implemented as defaults
ops.details: Modify IOStream declaration to extend IOStreamTerminal<T> in addition to IOBaseStream. Remove the listed default method implementations from IOStream (they are now provided by IOStreamTerminal). This keeps all moved behavior available to existing callers while making IOStream smaller and more focused. No change in method signatures visible to callers. Ensure imports in IOStream are updated if any become unused/needed.
